## On Pragmatic Engineering

> Premature abstraction is a bigger sin than a little duplication. Three similar lines of code is better than the wrong abstraction. The cost of fixing the wrong abstraction is much higher than the cost of duplication, because abstractions are sticky and they spread.
>
> — Sandi Metz, *Practical Object-Oriented Design*

> The first rule of optimization is don't. The second rule is don't yet. Profile before you decide what to optimize, because intuition about hot paths is wrong more often than it is right, and the actual hot paths are usually surprising.
>
> — Donald Knuth, *Computer Programming as an Art*
